overview:
## Discussion

You specify a position when creating a node, but you can also change a node’s position after initialization.

When working with an array of nodes returned by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method, read the position from each node to determine the path for an entity in your game scene to follow.

# position

**Instance Property**

The position of the node in continuous 2D space.

## Declaration

```swift
var position: vector_float2 { get set }
```

## Discussion

You specify a position when creating a node, but you can also change a node’s position after initialization.

When working with an array of nodes returned by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method, read the position from each node to determine the path for an entity in your game scene to follow.
title: position
